topic: Arjun wants a gaming console — should he get one

turns.content: right so Arjun has been going on and on about wanting a games console. every single day mate. all his mates at school have got one apparently. what do you reckon is it worth getting him one or am I opening pandoras box

It's a classic situation -- once the school friends all have one, the pressure builds fast. Whether it's worth it depends on a few things:

1. **How old is he?** Screen time concerns and game ratings are different for a 7-year-old vs a 12-year-old.
2. **What's he after?** A PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch -- they're quite different in what they offer.
3. **What's your main worry?** Is it the cost, the screen time, the type of games, or just the principle of it?

It's definitely manageable if you set the ground rules before you buy it rather than after. What's he been asking for specifically?

hes 9 now and he wants a playstation. hes been watching youtube videos of FIFA or whatever its called now and fortnite. my main worry is honestly the screen time cos once he gets on that thing hes not gonna want to do anything else is he. hes good at football and I dont want him turning into a couch potato

That's a totally fair concern. At 9 years old, the risk is real -- FIFA and Fortnite are designed to keep you playing. But here's the thing: most kids his age are going to encounter gaming whether it's at home or at their mates' houses. At least if it's in your house, you control the rules.

A few things to think about:

**Screen time controls:** The PS5 has built-in parental controls. You can set daily time limits, restrict what games he can access by age rating, and even set a bedtime cutoff. It literally locks him out when the time is up -- no arguments.

**Fortnite:** It's rated 12, so technically not for a 9-year-old. It's not violent in a graphic way, but it is competitive and some kids get properly wound up playing it. FIFA is fine for his age.

**Earning it:** Some parents tie console time to other activities -- an hour of football practice in the garden earns 30 minutes on the console. Keeps the balance.

**Cost:** A PS5 is around £450 new, or £350-380 secondhand. Games are £50-70 each, though FIFA and Fortnite (free to play) would keep him going for months.

Do you think you could stick to enforcing time limits, or would it become a daily battle?

the parental controls thing is brilliant I didnt know about that. if the console itself cuts him off then im not the bad guy innit. thats perfect. the earning it idea is good too actually cos hes got his football and if he stops going to practice cos hes playing fortnite then the console goes in the cupboard simple as

Exactly -- the console does the enforcing so you don't have to be the villain every night. You set it up once and it handles the cutoff. He'll moan at the screen, not at you.

The "console goes in the cupboard" threat is powerful too. The key is following through on it once. First time he skips football practice, it goes away for a week. He'll test it, kids always do. But once he knows you're serious, it won't be an issue.

A few practical tips if you do go for it:
- **Set the rules BEFORE you give it to him.** Write them down even. "One hour on school nights, two hours on weekends, football practice is non-negotiable."
- **Put the console in the living room, not his bedroom.** You can see what he's playing and it naturally limits late-night gaming.
- **Don't buy a headset straight away.** Online voice chat with strangers is the main concern with Fortnite at his age. Without a headset, he can still play but can't talk to randoms.

Are you thinking of this as a Christmas present or just a general purchase?

christmas yeah. good call on no headset actually I hadnt thought of that. some of the stuff people say online is proper nasty and hes 9. living room only is happening for sure. Simran already said that actually she said no way is it going in his room

Simran's already on it -- living room it is. She's right, bedroom gaming for a 9-year-old is a recipe for trouble. In the living room he's playing where everyone can see, and it naturally becomes a family thing rather than him disappearing for hours.

For Christmas, the timing is actually good. Black Friday is coming up in a couple of weeks and PS5 bundles often drop in price or come with a free game. Could save you £30-50 if you keep an eye out. Argos, Amazon, Currys, and GAME all usually do deals.

A solid Christmas setup would be:
- **PS5 console** (disc or digital -- digital is cheaper but you can't buy secondhand games)
- **EA FC** (the new FIFA) -- one game is enough to start with
- **Second controller** so you or Simran can play with him

The second controller is actually a great move. If you play FIFA with him, it becomes quality time rather than solo screen time.

oh mate if I start playing FIFA with him ill never get off it myself haha. nah but actually thats a proper good idea. Preet would want to play too though shes gonna kick off if Arjun gets to play and she doesnt. are there games for a 5 year old on playstation

Ha, FIFA has a way of pulling in the whole family! And yes, there are loads of games Preet could play:

- **Astro's Playroom** -- comes free with the PS5, it's colourful, simple controls, perfect for little ones
- **Sackboy: A Big Adventure** -- two-player co-op, really fun and not hard for younger kids
- **Lego games** -- any of them. Lego Star Wars, Lego Harry Potter, Lego Jurassic World. They're brilliant for kids her age and you can play together
- **Bluey: The Videogame** -- if she watches Bluey, she'll love it

The Lego Jurassic World one might be a winner if she's into dinosaurs at all. Two-player co-op so Arjun could play with her.

Honestly the PS5 could end up being a family thing rather than just Arjun's console, which makes the price easier to justify.

lego jurassic world is going straight on the list shes absolutely obsessed with dinosaurs. right I think im sold on this. black friday deal, living room only, parental controls on, no headset, football comes first. Simran cant argue with that plan can she

That's a bulletproof plan. You've covered every concern -- screen time, online safety, physical activity, cost, and Preet not feeling left out. If Simran can find a hole in that, she's a better negotiator than most lawyers.

Just remember: Black Friday deals go fast, especially on consoles. Set a price alert on Amazon or check HotUKDeals -- people post the best ones there as soon as they go live. And keep the box hidden well, because kids at that age turn into detectives in December.

Good luck with the purchase, and enjoy the family FIFA tournaments!

haha yeah hiding it is gonna be the hardest part. last year Arjun found his presents in the wardrobe a week early and we had to pretend we didnt know he knew. might stick it in the van this time he never goes in there

The van is a solid hiding spot — he's got no reason to rummage around in there. Just make sure it's wrapped well so it doesn't slide around and get damaged on the way to a job. Double box it if you can.
good shout yeah I'll wrap it up proper. right cheers for this mate genuinely helpful. cant believe I went from 'should I get him one' to a full battle plan in 10 minutes. sorted
That's what happens when you think it through properly — went from Pandora's box to a bulletproof Christmas plan. Enjoy the shopping and the big reveal on Christmas morning. Cheers!
